name: Centipede
description:
Centipede is a fixed shooter arcade game. The player controls a small character, referred to as the 'Shooter,' at the bottom of the screen. The primary objective is to defend against a descending centipede that winds its way down the playing field from the top. The centipede is composed of multiple segments, and each segment can be destroyed individually by shooting it. When a segment is hit, it transforms into a mushroom. If the centipede head encounters a mushroom, it will descend one level and reverse direction. If the centipede touches the Shooter's area at the bottom, the game ends. 

In addition to the centipede, there are other enemies that periodically appear and move across the screen. These enemies include fleas that drop vertically, leaving mushrooms in their wake; spiders that move erratically within the Shooter's area; and scorpions that poison any mushroom they touch. A poisoned mushroom will cause any centipede segment encountering it to rapidly descend to the Shooter's area. The game continues until the Shooter loses all lives, which occurs when the centipede reaches the Shooter's area and touches the Shooter. The gameplay area is a static screen where all the action takes place. The game progressively increases in difficulty as levels advance, often increasing the speed of the centipede and the frequency of the other enemies.

controls.arrow_left: Move the Shooter horizontally to the left.
controls.arrow_right: Move the Shooter horizontally to the right.
controls.arrow_up: Move the Shooter vertically upwards within the Shooter's area.
controls.arrow_down: Move the Shooter vertically downwards within the Shooter's area.
controls.space: Fire a shot upwards from the Shooter.
